factors.summary: The company is deleveraging by repaying fixed-rate senior notes and maintaining unused borrowing capacity.

evidence.text: We repaid the 3.200% Senior Notes on October 1, 2024. The Term Facility requires quarterly principal payments... We repaid $9.4 million of principal on the Term Facility during the nine months ended September 30, 2024.

factors.summary: Free cash flow has increased, supported by higher net earnings and lower cash used for working capital.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased $74.9 million compared to the same period in 2023, primarily driven by higher net earnings and lower cash used for working capital.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to grow, driven by investments in new product development and infrastructure.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities is used to invest in new product development and fund capital expenditures... Net cash used in investing activities increased $106.8 million compared to the same period in 2023, primarily due to cash used for acquisitions and higher capital expenditures.

factors.summary: Overall demand is growing, supported by increased volumes and favorable pricing.

evidence.text: In the third quarter of 2024, we experienced a mid-single digits percent increase in revenue as compared to the same period in 2023, driven by favorable pricing, volume growth from most of our major businesses, as well as the impact from acquisitions made during the year. We expect the business to continue to grow in the last quarter of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Business and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with positive customer responses contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased by 5.4%, or $49.2 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 1.8%, Volume 1.5%, Acquisitions 1.9%, Currency exchange rates 0.2%. The increase in Net revenues was driven by improved pricing, higher volumes, the acquisitions made during the year, and favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Operating margin for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased to 22.2% from 21.0%. The increase was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix, the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity, favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, and lower acquisition, integration, and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth alongside pricing.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased by 5.4%, or $49.2 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 1.8%, Volume 1.5%, Acquisitions 1.9%, Currency exchange rates 0.2%. The increase in Net revenues was driven by improved pricing, higher volumes, the acquisitions made during the year, and favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: The non-residential market is growing due to higher volumes and improved pricing, while the residential market is also showing low-single digits growth.

evidence.text: Net revenues from our non-residential business for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased by a mid-single digits percent compared to the same period in the prior year, driven by higher volumes, improved pricing, and acquisition activity, which was partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements. Net revenues from residential products for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased by a low-single digits percent compared to the same period in the prior year, driven by higher volumes.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: The increase in Operating income and Operating margin was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix, the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity, favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, and lower acquisition, integration, and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, contributing to higher margins and cost savings.

evidence.text: Pricing and productivity in excess of inflation and investment spending includes the impact to both Operating income and Operating margin from pricing, as defined above, in addition to productivity, inflation and investment spending. Productivity represents improvements in unit costs of materials, cost reductions related to improvements to our manufacturing design and processes and reductions in selling and administrative expenses due to productivity projects.

factors.summary: Management is confident with the business outlook, as evidenced by their expectation of continued growth and successful integration of acquisitions.

evidence.text: We expect the business to continue to grow in the last quarter of 2024. The increase in Operating income and Operating margin was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix, the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity, favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, and lower acquisition, integration, and restructuring expenses.
evidence.subsection: Business and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The company is executing a clear long-term strategy focused on acquisitions and market expansion, as evidenced by recent purchases.

evidence.text: On February 1, 2024, we, through our subsidiaries, acquired 100% of Boss Door Controls... On March 4, 2024, we acquired 100% of Montajes electronicos Dorcas S.L. ... On June 3, 2024, we acquired 100% of Krieger Specialty Products, LLC ... On June 10, 2024, we acquired 100% of Unicel Architectural Corp.

factors.summary: Currency exchange rates and foreign exchange movements are significant factors impacting the company's results.

evidence.text: Currency exchange rates 0.2% ... The increase in interest income for the three months ended September 30, 2024 compared with the same period in 2023 is due to higher cash on hand and higher interest rates earned on deposits.

factors.summary: Recent acquisitions are acting as catalysts for growth, contributing to both revenue and operating income.

evidence.text: The increase in Net revenues was driven by improved pricing, higher volumes, the acquisitions made during the year, and favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements. The increase in Operating income and Operating margin was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix, the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity, favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, and lower acquisition, integration, and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as global minimum tax, are impacting the company's effective tax rate.

evidence.text: The effective income tax rates for the three months ended September 30, 2024 and 2023, were 10.5% and 8.1%, respectively. The increase in the effective income tax rate compared to 2023 is primarily due to the enactment of global minimum tax in 2024, the unfavorable year over year change in the amounts recognized for uncertain tax positions and the unfavorable mix of income earned in higher tax rate jurisdictions.

factors.summary: The economic environment is favorable, supporting growth in key end markets.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased by 5.4%, or $49.2 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 1.8%, Volume 1.5%, Acquisitions 1.9%, Currency exchange rates 0.2%. The increase in Net revenues was driven by improved pricing, higher volumes, the acquisitions made during the year, and favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are positively impacting the company's results through favorable translation.

evidence.text: Currency exchange rates 0.2% ... The increase in interest income for the three months ended September 30, 2024 compared with the same period in 2023 is due to higher cash on hand and higher interest rates earned on deposits.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive margin trajectory with both gross and operating margins expanding, supported by strong cash flows and financial flexibility.

evidence.text: Our business operates with strong operating cash flows, low leverage and low capital intensity, providing financial flexibility... We expect our cash flows from operations will be sufficient... to meet our current financing needs for at least the next 12 months.

Operating margin for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased to 22.2% from 21.0%. The increase was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix, the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity, favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, and lower acquisition, integration, and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: The company is actively returning value to shareholders through both dividends and share repurchases, reflecting confidence in its growth prospects. However, the increase in cash used for share repurchases partially offsets the financing activities' cash inflow.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by financing activities increased $367.2 million... primarily due to the proceeds from the issuance of the 5.600% Senior Notes. The increase was partially offset by an increase in the cash used to repurchase shares compared to the same period in 2023.
During the nine months ended September 30, 2024, we paid dividends of $1.44 per ordinary share to shareholders and repurchased approximately 0.9 million shares for $120.0 million.

factors.summary: The company faces mixed impacts from interest rate changes. While 91% of its debt is fixed-rate, leading to mitigation of rising variable rates, there is an increase in both interest expense and income due to higher rates and outstanding indebtedness.

evidence.text: Of our total outstanding indebtedness as of September 30, 2024, approximately 91% incurs fixed-rate interest and is therefore not exposed to the risk of rising variable interest rates.

Interest expense for the three months ended September 30, 2024, increased $5.9 million compared with the same period in 2023, primarily due to higher outstanding indebtedness compared to the same period in the prior year. The increase in interest income for the three months ended September 30, 2024 compared with the same period in 2023 is due to higher cash on hand and higher interest rates earned on deposits.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated significant merger and acquisition (M&A) activity, as evidenced by multiple acquisitions throughout 2024, which are aimed at driving growth and expanding capabilities. However, this increased activity has resulted in higher capital expenditures and cash usage in investing activities, potentially impacting liquidity.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities increased $106.8 million compared to the same period in 2023, primarily due to cash used for acquisitions and higher capital expenditures.
On February 1, 2024, we, through our subsidiaries, acquired 100% of Boss Door Controls...
On March 4, 2024, we acquired 100% of Montajes electronicos Dorcas S.L. ...
On June 3, 2024, we acquired 100% of Krieger Specialty Products, LLC ...
On June 10, 2024, we acquired 100% of Unicel Architectural Corp.
